name: Construct Mapping review for Validity Conditions and Controlled Comparison Update bundle
summary: Accepted. The staged artifacts keep response origin, label source, comparator, evaluation method, and scorer role separate. The SMART record distinguishes generated responses, human-scored real responses, an LLM scoring model, and IRT item-difficulty estimates; the other records remain realism/authenticity evidence rather than scorer-validation evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged records do not conflate synthetic-response realism with scorer validity or real-response interchangeability.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the central risk in the research question: plausible responses can be validity-relevant without being sufficient validation evidence.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the explicit scorer_use, real_response_comparator, and limitations fields in each staged artifact.
